coverage_profile.seniority_signal: Partner and Group Head of the Consumer Sector (most recent title) — a senior coverage role; management engages her directly and by name across multiple calls, and she asks multi-part, strategic questions, consistent with senior analyst interaction (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2025-Q3).
coverage_profile.sector_lens: Covers consumer-facing travel/leisure with a focus on cruise dynamics — passenger sourcing, onboard revenue, booking behavior and branded/owned-destination products (Perfect Day/Royal Beach Clubs).
rhetorical_signature: Opens politely ("Hi. Good morning.") and references prior calls or data points (e.g., "on the last call" asking about the 80% North American stat in 2024-Q1).
Frequently asks two-part or follow-up questions that link customer segments to revenue outcomes (seen in 2024-Q1 follow-up and 2025-Q3 question on ticket lift vs onboard spend).
Tone is collegial and concise; she frames questions around customer cohorts, booking behavior, and product-driven yield rather than operational minutiae.

reasoning_style.justification: Her questions blend quantitative anchors (the 80% North American figure, ‘1 in 2 passengers being Millennials’) with strategic/qualitative probes about booking behavior and product impacts (Perfect Day/Royal Beach Clubs).
reasoning_style.follow_up_pattern: Tends to ask multi-part questions and explicit follow-ups (Turn 1 is two-part: current mix and next year expectations; Turn 2 is a stated follow-up on pre-book behavior). She does sometimes stop after a short acknowledgement, but generally pursues cause-and-effect answers linking customer mix to revenue.
reasoning_style.evidence_demanded: She seeks specific updates (percent sourcing, changes year-over-year), behavioral evidence (pre-booking tendencies by cohort), and mechanistic explanations of how products change yield/ticket lift (e.g., CocoCay/Perfect Day vs Royal Beach Clubs).
reasoning_style.anchoring_habits: Frequently anchors to prior calls/guidance (explicitly references the prior call's 80% stat in 2024-Q1) and to concrete product/brand comparators (Perfect Day/CocoCay). She also frames questions around customer cohorts (Millennials vs older guests) rather than peers, though peer comparisons are not present in the record.

core_topics.what_they_press_on: Wants updates on the geographic composition (e.g., North American share) and expectations for next-year shifts (China/Europe ramp) and how that influences revenue patterns.

core_topics.topic: Booking behavior and pre-book penetration by demographic
core_topics.what_they_press_on: Probes whether younger cohorts (Millennials) pre-book more than older cohorts and whether improved digital/frictionless experiences are driving pre-booking across demographics.

core_topics.topic: Owned-destination/product impact on yield and ticket lift
core_topics.what_they_press_on: Asks how branded/owned destinations (CocoCay/Perfect Day; Royal Beach Clubs) shift the mix between ticket lift and onboard/excursion spend and how they affect itinerary demand.

recurring_concerns.blind_spots: In the available history she does not press on unit costs, fuel/margin drivers, balance-sheet/capital allocation, or competitive comparisons to peers; however, the sample is small (four turns), so absence of these topics may reflect limited opportunities rather than deliberate avoidance.
recurring_concerns.stance_drift: Based on the sparse record, she moved from probing passenger mix and demographic booking behavior in 2024-Q1 to product-level questions about revenue composition and itinerary demand from owned destinations by 2025-Q3, indicating a shift from recovery/customer mix questions toward yield composition and product ROI. Evidence is limited to the provided calls.
